title: Amdocs and Google Cloud Announce Partnership Expansion to Optimize 5G Network Operations with AI
summary: Amdocs and Google Cloud are expanding their partnership to optimize 5G network operations using AI. Amdocs will launch its Network AIOps solution, built on Google Cloud's Vertex AI and BigQuery, to help telecommunications companies automate operations, enhance reliability, and improve customer experiences. This collaboration aims to address the complexities of 5G networks by providing AI-driven insights, automated workflows, closed-loop automation, and predictive maintenance.

title: Chegg Reports 2024 Fourth Quarter and Full Year Financial Results
summary: Chegg, Inc. has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2024, showing a significant decrease in revenue and subscribers. The company is pursuing a strategic review process to maximize shareholder value and has filed a lawsuit against Google LLC and Alphabet Inc., alleging that Google's AI Overviews have negatively impacted its traffic and revenue. Chegg anticipates continued revenue decline in the first quarter of 2025 but aims to stabilize its business through brand awareness, product expansion, and diversification.

title: Paul Harris' Top Picks: Alphabet, Novo Nordisk & MSCI Inc
summary: Paul Harris, a portfolio manager at Harris Douglas Asset Management, has identified Alphabet, Novo Nordisk, and MSCI Inc. as his top stock picks to watch in the market. The article highlights his selections, providing insights from a financial expert for potential investors.

title: Ooma vs Google Voice: Which VoIP System is Superior?
summary: This article compares Ooma and Google Voice, concluding that Ooma is generally superior due to its features, customer support, and integrations, although Google Voice is more affordable for small teams and offers better security. The comparison details pricing, features, integrations, security, customer support, and international calling rates to help businesses choose the best VoIP system for their needs.

title: NetApp, Inc. (NTAP): Among Stocks That Analysts Think Will Go Up
summary: NetApp, Inc. (NASDAQ:NTAP) was recently upgraded from Underperform to Neutral by analysts, with a price target increase from $121 to $128. This positive outlook is driven by increasing demand for public cloud services due to AI infrastructure spending, which expands NetApp's Total Addressable Market. The company's stable gross margins (71%-72%) and expected growth in operating margins (around 30%) with healthy free cash flows contribute to its solid growth prospects, positioning it well among stocks analysts believe will rise.

title: Spotify partners with ElevenLabs to expand its library of AI-narrated audiobooks
summary: Spotify has partnered with ElevenLabs to significantly expand its collection of AI-narrated audiobooks. This collaboration allows authors to use ElevenLabs' advanced AI voice technology to narrate their books in 29 languages and then upload them to Spotify via Findaway Voices, after a review process. While this move is expected to boost the number of AI-narrated titles, it also anticipates debate within the publishing community regarding the potential impact on audiobook quality.

title: Google and AutoNation partner on self-driving car program
summary: Google's Waymo, its automated vehicle technology arm, has partnered with AutoNation, the largest auto dealership chain in the U.S., for maintenance and repair services for its self-driving car fleet. The multi-year deal will initially cover Chrysler Pacifica vehicles and extend to future models. This partnership highlights the increasing collaborations between tech giants and automotive companies in the race to develop widely available self-driving technology.

title: Why NeoGenomics (NEO) Shares Are Trading Lower Today
summary: NeoGenomics (NASDAQ:NEO) shares dropped significantly after reporting weak fourth-quarter results, with revenue below Wall Street expectations and the company remaining unprofitable with negative free cash flow. Despite the challenging quarter, full-year sales and EBITDA guidance were more encouraging, surpassing consensus estimates. The stock is highly volatile and has been down 26.3% since the beginning of the year.

title: Baidu Stock Slips After Dip In Advertising Revenue Overshadows Q4 Earnings Beat: Retail’s Still Impressed
summary: Baidu's stock (BIDU) declined despite beating Q4 2024 earnings estimates, primarily due to a 7% drop in advertising revenue, which overshadowed its overall financial performance. The company's CEO, Robin Li, highlighted a strategic shift towards an AI-first business, with strong growth in AI Cloud and its Apollo Go robotaxi service. Despite the stock slip, retail sentiment on platforms like Stocktwits remained "extremely bullish," with some investors considering the stock undervalued given its AI advancements.

title: The shrinking public market: What investors need to know
summary: The article discusses the shrinking public market and the increasing prominence of private equity. It highlights that due to structural forces and the rise of the intangible economy, many companies are staying private longer, leading to a decline in listed companies and later access to growth for public market investors. The authors argue that including private equity in investment portfolios can offer diversification and access to earlier-stage growth opportunities that are increasingly absent from public markets.

title: Reddit price target raised to $222 from $187 at B. Riley
summary: B. Riley has increased its price target for Reddit (RDDT) shares to $222 from $187, while maintaining a Buy rating. This adjustment follows Reddit's stronger-than-expected Q4 results, driven by its advertising business. The firm dismissed concerns about a slight slowdown in daily active user growth, attributing it to a late Q4 Google search ranking change.

title: Google, SoftBank invest in QuEra's neutral atom quantum computer project - CHOSUNBIZ
summary: Google and SoftBank have made investments in QuEra, a company focused on developing neutral atom quantum computers. This collaboration signifies major backing for QuEra's quantum computing initiatives.

title: Comcast’s DEI policies face federal scrutiny as FCC opens investigation
summary: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) has launched an investigation into Comcast Corp. and its subsidiary NBCUniversal regarding their Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) policies. FCC Chairman Brendan Carr, a Trump appointee, stated concerns that the companies might be promoting "invidious forms of discrimination" in violation of civil rights laws, following an executive order from President Trump to combat such initiatives in the private sector. Comcast has indicated it will cooperate with the FCC's inquiry.

title: DeepSeek Shows There Is No Longer One “AI Trade,” but Many
summary: Recent developments from Chinese company DeepSeek, particularly its R1 model which uses fewer expensive chips, are signaling a new phase in AI investing. Fund managers now anticipate greater divergence among AI players, with potential benefits for companies like Broadcom and Marvell due to a shift towards more efficient chips. While efficiency gains could impact demand for some hardware, the overall growth in AI applications is expected to compensate, benefiting key players like Nvidia and Taiwan Semiconductor.

title: After Earnings, Is Alphabet Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar maintains a 4-star "undervalued" rating for Alphabet stock, raising its fair value estimate to $237 per share after solid Q4 earnings. The tech giant's core advertising business and Google Cloud growth, despite some deceleration, are seen as strong drivers, backed by a wide economic moat and robust financial health. Morningstar views Alphabet's comprehensive AI strategy and recent stock selloff as a buying opportunity, forecasting continued growth and stable operating margins.

title: Is Alphabet a No-Brainer Bargain Buy Right Now?
summary: Alphabet's shares recently dropped after its quarterly revenue missed analysts' estimates and capital spending plans exceeded predictions, leading to investor disappointment. Despite this, the company's core Google Services and Google Cloud segments showed double-digit revenue growth and significant AI advancements. With a strong track record and market leadership, the current valuation at 20x forward earnings makes Alphabet a potential bargain buy for long-term investors.

title: Alphabet (Google) Stock Price Dropped by 10%: Should You Buy?
summary: Alphabet Inc.'s stock price dropped by 10% after its Q4 FY 2024 earnings report, despite strong growth across all segments. Concerns revolve around slightly lower-than-expected Google Cloud revenue growth and anticipated increases in depreciation and capital expenditure. The article analyzes these concerns, suggesting the dip might present an investment opportunity given the company's strong financial position and the rapidly expanding AI market.

title: Should You Own Both QQQ and VOO?
summary: This article evaluates whether investors should own both the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO). It concludes that owning both is likely not beneficial due to significant overlap in holdings and similar performance, with QQQ introducing greater downside risk primarily because of its heavy concentration in technology stocks. The author suggests QQQ is better viewed as an active bet on the tech sector rather than a diversification tool.

title: Is WPP living proof that Big Tech’s multi-billion bet on AI will pay off?
summary: WPP's chief technology officer, Stephan Pretorius, and Google Cloud's CMO, Alison Wagonfeld, discuss Big Tech's significant investment in AI and its application in WPP's AI-powered marketing platform, WPP Open. Despite investor scrutiny and concerns about the speed of AI adoption, WPP has invested over $300m in its platform, integrating various AI and cloud solutions, and reshaping its business model by blurring traditional marketing silos. The article suggests that WPP's comprehensive AI integration serves as a strong use case for Big Tech's multi-billion dollar bet on artificial intelligence.

title: Militia Long/Short Equity ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (ORR) $ORR
summary: This article provides an aggregate rating and price target for the Militia Long/Short Equity ETF (ORR), which currently holds a "Moderate Buy" rating with an aggregate price target of $35.81. The rating is based on 218 analyst evaluations covering 14 companies within its portfolio over the past year. The article also lists the top 11 holdings of the ETF, including Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, Energy Transfer, and Amazon.com, along with their individual ratings and potential upside.

title: Rezolve Ai Kicks Off 2025 with Major Retail Expansion as Leading Brands Leverage Its Cutting-Edge Technology
summary: Rezolve Ai is experiencing significant growth in early 2025, with major brands like Dunkin', BJ's Wholesale Club, Coles Supermarkets, and the Phoenix Suns adopting its AI-powered retail and commerce solutions. The company's technology is reaching over 16 million mobile devices monthly, enhancing customer engagement and operational efficiency through features like drive-thru detection, curbside pickup, and automated check-ins. Strategic partnerships with Microsoft and Google are expected to further accelerate Rezolve Ai's expansion and impact on personalized retail experiences.

title: Alphabet Inc. (GOOG) reports earnings
summary: Alphabet Inc. (GOOG) reported its fiscal year 2024 earnings, with revenues reaching $350.0 billion, a 14% increase from the previous year, driven by Google Services and Google Cloud. The company achieved a net income of $100.1 billion and diluted earnings per share of $8.04. Alphabet also authorized an additional $70.0 billion for share repurchases and initiated a quarterly cash dividend program.

title: Ford CFO swap: Sherry House, GM engineer turned investment banker, rises
summary: Ford Motor Co. has announced Sherry House will replace John Lawler as Chief Financial Officer, effective February 6, 2025. House brings a unique background from product engineering at General Motors to investment banking and CFO roles at Lucid Motors and Waymo, highlighting her passion for innovation and its funding. Lawler will transition to vice chair, focusing on strategic development and global partnerships.

title: Spencer Rascoff Appointed Match Group Chief Executive Officer
summary: Match Group has announced the appointment of Spencer Rascoff as its new Chief Executive Officer, effective immediately. Rascoff, co-founder and former CEO of Zillow Group, succeeds Bernard Kim and brings extensive experience in building and scaling consumer technology businesses. His appointment signals a continued focus on innovation, including AI-powered features, to enhance user experiences across Match Group's portfolio of dating apps.

title: AppLovin: Another Short-Seller Report
summary: Morningstar maintains its fair value estimate of $284 per share for AppLovin despite a recent short-seller report from Muddy Waters Research. The report alleges that AppLovin overstates return on ad spending and violates platform terms of service by creating persistent identity graphs (PIGs). While shares fell 20% on the news, Morningstar highlights the "Very High Uncertainty Rating" but states some claims like aggressive retargeting seem misplaced, focusing on the allegation of unauthorized proprietary ID aggregation.

title: Nuclear startup Terrestrial Energy goes public via SPAC, netting $280M in merger
summary: Terrestrial Energy, a nuclear startup based in North Carolina, has merged with a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC), raising $280 million. The company, which develops integral molten salt reactors (IMSRs), plans to list on Nasdaq under the symbol IMSR. This move follows the increasing interest in nuclear power as a solution to rising electricity demand, particularly from data centers and AI operations.

title: AT&T In Talks To Buy Lumen Technologies’ Consumer Fiber Unit Amid Lumen’s AI Push: Report
summary: AT&T is reportedly in discussions to acquire Lumen Technologies’ consumer fiber operations, a deal that could be valued at over $5.5 billion. This potential sale aligns with Lumen's strategy to divest non-profitable business units and focus on its enterprise services, particularly in building infrastructure for AI, which now accounts for 75 percent of its revenue. Lumen's CEO, Kate Johnson, has emphasized growing the company by providing the backbone for AI, recently securing $8.5 billion in private connectivity deals.

title: Can Nuclear Energy Help Big Tech Meet Data Center Needs?
summary: The growing electricity demands of data centers, particularly due to AI, are leading Big Tech companies to consider nuclear energy, including small modular reactors (SMRs). While wind and solar are carbon-free, they don't provide the consistent power required, and natural gas, though quick and cheap, has carbon emissions. However, SMRs are expensive and take a long time to come online, posing challenges for utilities trying to meet this new demand while managing costs and timelines.

title: Palo Alto Networks CEO describes cybersecurity risk with agentic AI
summary: Palo Alto Networks CEO Nikesh Arora warns of new cybersecurity challenges posed by agentic AI, which gives AI "arms and legs" to make decisions, citing robotaxis as an example. He explained to Jim Cramer that as AI agents manage critical infrastructure and human intervention decreases, the "attack surface" for hackers expands, necessitating protection of access points. Arora also sees automation and efficiency driven by technology as a big opportunity for government entities to reduce costs.

title: Google defeats part of US shareholder class action over digital ads
summary: A federal judge in San Francisco dismissed part of a shareholder class-action lawsuit against Alphabet's Google. The lawsuit accused Google of misleading investors about its digital advertising practices and user privacy. While the judge ruled that claims based on website statements lacked adequate support, she allowed a claim concerning a 2020 statement by CEO Sundar Pichai to Congress to proceed.

title: Cloud GIS Market Set for Strong Growth Outlook: Esri, Google, Pitney Bowes
summary: The Cloud GIS market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 14.4% from 2025 to 2031, driven by increasing adoption of cloud computing, integration with AI and IoT, and growth in smart city initiatives. Key players in this market include Esri, Google, Hexagon AB, Autodesk, Trimble Inc., and Pitney Bowes. The market study highlights trends, drivers, and opportunities, emphasizing the shift towards scalable, on-demand geospatial solutions.

title: Google’s landlord lists tech firm’s Venice campus for sale
summary: Net Lease Office Properties, a spin-off of W.P. Carey, is listing Google's Venice campus, known as the Binoculars Building, for sale through CBRE. The 78,578-square-foot property's triple-net-lease with Google is up for renewal in October 2030. This sale comes after W.P. Carey's 2023 decision to spin off 59 office properties, and follows Google's reversal on its L.A. office expansion last year.

title: Youth mental health: Corpus Christi ISD seeks to join lawsuit against social media companies
summary: Corpus Christi ISD's board of trustees has voted to join an ongoing federal lawsuit against major social media companies, alleging that their platforms contribute to a youth mental health crisis and disrupt the learning environment. The district will only pay legal fees if the lawsuit results in a successful judgment or settlement, aligning with approximately 800 other school districts and 46 Texas school districts awaiting attorney general approval to participate. This action reflects growing concerns from health officials and educators, highlighting issues like addiction, bullying, and the need for increased mental health support in schools due to social media use.

title: Build and deploy Remote Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers to Cloudflare
summary: Cloudflare is enabling developers to build and deploy remote Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, moving beyond local-only implementations. The platform now offers tools like workers-oauth-provider for easy authorization, McpAgent for remote transport, and mcp-remote to connect existing local MCP clients to remote servers. This shift aims to broaden the accessibility of AI agents and LLMs to a wider audience, including for everyday consumer use cases, by making MCP servers internet-accessible and handling complex authentication and transport details.

title: Equities in 2025: Embracing a Broader Landscape
summary: Goldman Sachs Asset Management foresees a broadening landscape of equity market opportunities for active investors in 2025, moving beyond the dominance of US mega-cap tech. The report highlights potential in small-cap companies, developed markets like Japan and Europe, and emerging markets such as India and specific sectors in China. Investors are encouraged to pursue diversified, active strategies and explore long-term themes like reshoring and increased energy demand.

title: Quest Diagnostics Strikes Generative AI Partnership with Google Cloud
summary: Quest Diagnostics has partnered with Google Cloud to integrate generative AI capabilities into its services, aiming to enhance predictive population health analytics, customize physician reporting, and improve patient engagement. This collaboration will leverage Google Cloud's AI technologies, such as Google Agentspace, to generate personalized health insights, streamline access to comprehensive laboratory data for clinicians, and boost reporting efficiency through natural-language prompts. The initiative also seeks to uncover subtle patterns in data for faster diagnoses and personalized medicine.

title: Rob Arnott on Market Inefficiencies and Investing Opportunities
summary: Rob Arnott, founder of Research Affiliates, discusses market inefficiencies and investing opportunities, focusing on the behavioral mistakes of performance chasing and impatience. He elaborates on the impact of user-friendly AI, the nature of market narratives, and how index investing creates exploitable inefficiencies. Arnott also addresses the growth versus value debate, arguing that value stocks can be as growth-oriented as growth stocks.

title: Quest, Google team on gen AI; GE Healthcare plans autonomous imaging
summary: Medtech companies showcased their latest AI advancements at Nvidia’s GTC 2025 conference. Quest Diagnostics announced a partnership with Google Cloud to leverage generative AI for data streamlining and personalized customer experiences. GE Healthcare expanded its collaboration with Nvidia to develop autonomous X-ray and ultrasound systems aimed at alleviating staff shortages and improving imaging workflows.

title: Google to set sail in 35-story downtown Austin tower near Lady Bird Lake this year
summary: Google has confirmed it will finally move into its 35-story sail-shaped tower in downtown Austin this year, after several delays due to the shift to remote and hybrid work environments. The company signed a lease for the entire building overlooking Lady Bird Lake six years ago, and real estate investment trust Cousins Property recently acquired the tower for over $520 million. This move is significant for the Austin office market, which has faced rising vacancy rates, indicating a bullish long-term outlook despite ongoing challenges.

title: Eschew Magnificent Seven Stock-Picking With This ETF
summary: This article suggests that investors consider actively managed ETFs like the Invesco Top QQQ ETF (QBIG) instead of trying to pick individual Magnificent Seven stocks. The ETF focuses on the Nasdaq-100 Mega Index, which includes the Magnificent Seven plus Broadcom, offering flexibility in the current market. The article highlights that the Magnificent Seven's valuation is at an eight-year low relative to the S&P 500, with companies like Amazon and Alphabet presenting strong potential for growth.

title: NVIDIA Teams Up with GE, Google, IBM, And CrowdStrike To Redefine AI And Quantum Technology
summary: NVIDIA is establishing the NVIDIA Accelerated Quantum Research Center (NVAQC) in Boston and announced multiple collaborations to advance AI and quantum computing. Partnerships include Google for AI initiatives across various industries, GE HealthCare for autonomous medical imaging, IBM for enterprise AI capabilities, and CrowdStrike for AI-driven cybersecurity. Additionally, NVIDIA is working with General Motors on AI-powered vehicles and Yum! Brands to revolutionize AI in the restaurant industry.

title: Magnificent 7 Stocks Are Falling: How Low Can They Go?
summary: The author discusses the current downturn in the Magnificent 7 stocks, viewing it as a market correction rather than a crash, with most stocks trading 20-25% off their all-time highs. He analyzes the technical indicators of the Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF (MAGS) and provides specific insights and support levels for Meta Platforms (META), Alphabet (GOOG), and Microsoft (MSFT), highlighting potential buy opportunities.

title: Where can investors find defensive assets in today’s evolving markets?
summary: This article explores how investors can identify defensive assets in today's changing market environment, characterized by evolving risks like inflation and varied growth scenarios. T. Rowe Price experts discuss the continued role of the U.S. dollar, the renewed attractiveness of cash due to higher yields, and the changing dynamics of long-maturity U.S. Treasuries. They also touch on the potential defensive qualities of commodities, certain equity sectors like healthcare and consumer staples, and the re-evaluation of the "Magnificent Seven" as a defensive asset class.

title: NVIDIA, Alphabet and Google Collaborate on the Future of Agentic and Physical AI
summary: NVIDIA, Alphabet, and Google are expanding their collaboration to advance AI, focusing on agentic AI, robotics, and broader accessibility of AI tools. Their joint efforts include optimizing open models like Gemma, developing advanced robotics capabilities through Intrinsic, and applying AI to drug discovery with Isomorphic Labs and grid optimization with X's Tapestry. Google Cloud will also adopt NVIDIA's latest Blackwell GPUs to power these research and production initiatives.

title: Anthropic Enterprise Edge: How Investors Can Gain Exposure Through The AGIX ETF
summary: This article highlights Anthropic's significant position in the AI sector and its ethical approach to AI development. It explains how investors can gain exposure to this private AI company through the KraneShares Artificial Intelligence & Technology ETF (AGIX), which holds Anthropic as its fourth-largest holding. The piece also details Anthropic's rapid growth, strategic partnerships with tech giants like Amazon, Google, Salesforce, and AWS, and the launch of its advanced Claude 3.7 Sonnet AI model.

title: NVIDIA Announces Isaac GR00T N1 — the World’s First Open Humanoid Robot Foundation Model — and Simulation Frameworks to Speed Robot Development
summary: NVIDIA announced Isaac GR00T N1, the world’s first open and customizable foundation model for humanoid robots, along with new simulation frameworks like the Isaac GR00T Blueprint and the Newton physics engine. These technologies are designed to accelerate humanoid robot development, making them more adaptable and capable of complex tasks. NVIDIA is collaborating with Google DeepMind and Disney Research on the Newton engine, and the GR00T N1 model is already being adopted by leading humanoid developers.

title: Alphabet’s John Hennessy sells shares worth over $33,000
summary: John L. Hennessy, a director at Alphabet Inc., recently sold 200 shares of the company's Class C Capital Stock for approximately $33,106. The transaction was conducted under a pre-arranged 10b5-1 trading plan. Despite the sale, InvestingPro analysis suggests Alphabet is currently undervalued, with strong financial health indicators.

title: CrowdStrike & Google Cloud: AI-driven cloud security
summary: CrowdStrike Holdings Inc. and Google LLC are collaborating to deliver AI-powered cloud security solutions at scale, addressing critical enterprise challenges in cloud adoption. This partnership leverages CrowdStrike's cloud-native security platform and AI-driven threat intelligence with Google Cloud's infrastructure and Marketplace to enhance protection, streamline procurement, and accelerate security deployment for businesses. The integration aims to simplify security, allowing enterprises to confidently build and operate in the cloud while effectively stopping breaches.

title: Rackspace Targeted in Latest CL0P Ransomware Attack
summary: The CL0P ransomware group has claimed responsibility for a cyberattack against cloud storage provider Rackspace Technology, beginning to leak stolen files on March 11, 2025. This breach reportedly involved the exploitation of zero-day vulnerabilities in Cleo file transfer software, following a broader campaign impacting over 170 organizations. Rackspace, which has faced previous cyberattacks, is now under renewed scrutiny regarding its cybersecurity practices as the group’s actions highlight persistent supply chain attack threats and the critical need for timely vulnerability management.

title: Sola Security aims to be the Wix of cyber with $30 million Seed round
summary: Sola Security, founded by Guy Flechter and Ron Peled, has emerged from stealth mode with a total of $30 million in Seed funding. The startup's platform aims to simplify cybersecurity for small businesses and tech startups, allowing professionals to build tailored security tools, much like Wix revolutionized website building. The platform will initially be free, and the funding includes an additional $2 million from angel investors, supplementing an earlier $28 million round led by S Capital, Michael Moritz, and S32 fund.

title: Why FCF ETF 'VFLO' Looks Strong in 2025
summary: The VictoryShares Free Cash Flow ETF (VFLO) demonstrated strong demand in 2024 and is well-positioned for 2025, offering diversification, attractive valuations, and strong historical returns. VFLO targets large-cap U.S. companies with high free cash flow yields, excluding "Magnificent Seven" stocks, making it a valuable complement to broad benchmarks. Its performance in 2024, generating a nearly 22% return, highlights its potential to improve value exposure in portfolios.

title: Vodafone: Be in the office 8 days a month or lose bonuses
summary: Vodafone is mandating its UK employees to work in the office at least eight days a month, or face disciplinary action and potential loss of bonuses starting April 1. This policy, in place since 2021, is now being strictly enforced, despite staff concerns about reduced office space and the need to collaborate with remote international teams via video calls. The company emphasizes the importance of in-office collaboration while employees point to research questioning the productivity benefits of RTO mandates and the impact on morale.

title: Google stock hits all-time high at 226.43 USD
summary: Alphabet Inc.'s Class C shares (GOOG) reached an all-time high of $226.43, boosting its market capitalization to $2.56 trillion, reflecting a 34.33% increase over the past year. Despite technical indicators suggesting the stock is in overbought territory, analysts maintain a strong buy consensus with a highest price target of $270. Recent positive developments, including favorable court rulings and expansion plans for Waymo, have contributed to investor confidence.

title: Salesforce joins growing list of companies ending diversity hiring initiatives
summary: Salesforce Inc. has become the latest company to end its diversity hiring initiatives, removing language about diversity and inclusion from its annual financial disclosure. This decision follows executive orders signed by President Donald Trump targeting the elimination of DEI programs. Other major companies like Google, Meta, Target, Amazon, Walmart, and Deloitte have also rolled back similar initiatives.

title: Are Google Ads Worth It? When and Why They Don’t Work
summary: This article examines the effectiveness of Google Ads in 2025, outlining when they work best and when they prove to be ineffective. It highlights that Google Ads are most suitable for companies with substantial budgets, strong online presences, and clearly defined target audiences. Conversely, they are less effective for small businesses, those in highly competitive fields, or brands needing immediate results, often leading to budget overruns and diminishing returns due to audience fatigue.

title: Google Cloud, Project Innerspace and SLB Collaborate to Accelerate Global Geothermal Energy Adoption
summary: Google Cloud, SLB, and Project Innerspace are collaborating to boost global geothermal energy adoption. This partnership combines Project Innerspace's GeoMap, SLB's GeothermEx consulting services, and Google Cloud's infrastructure to identify and develop geothermal resources more efficiently. Geothermal energy holds significant potential to meet global electricity demands as a clean and firm power source.

title: Carrier, Google Partner for Energy Management Initiative
summary: Carrier Global Corp. and Google Cloud are collaborating to enhance grid flexibility and smart energy management. This partnership will integrate Carrier Energy's battery-enabled HVAC technology with Google Cloud's AI-powered analytics and WeatherNext AI models to create intelligent, connected solutions for residential HVAC energy customers. The initiative aims to help balance electricity supply and demand, enable homeowners to store and use energy during peak demand, and support both companies' goals for carbon-free energy solutions and greenhouse gas emissions reduction.

title: Malaysia to pay Arm Holdings $250 million for chip design blueprints
summary: Malaysia will pay Arm Holdings $250 million over 10 years for chip design blueprints to enable local manufacturers to produce their own chips within the next decade, driven by the AI boom. Arm will also establish its first Southeast Asian office in Kuala Lumpur, and the deal includes training 10,000 engineers in Malaysia. This initiative aims to build a complete supply chain in advanced industries like AI data servers, autonomous vehicles, and robotics, and to foster 10 local chip companies with significant yearly revenue.

title: Alphabet’s chief accounting officer sells $238k in stock
summary: Alphabet's Chief Accounting Officer, Amie Thuener O’Toole, sold 1,374 shares of Class C Capital Stock totaling approximately $238,347. This transaction was part of a Rule 10b5-1 Trading Plan. Alphabet maintains strong financial health, with robust cash flows and recent updates in personalized ads policy, AI development, and office attendance requirements for employees.

title: 19 Affiliate Marketing Tools to Boost Conversions
summary: This article lists 19 essential affiliate marketing tools to help boost conversions, covering various aspects like SEO, social media management, email marketing, and content creation. Each tool is described with its features, pricing, pros, and cons. The article also provides best practices for choosing and leveraging these tools effectively, emphasizing goal setting, avoiding overlap, and consistency.

title: Alphabet Sets Positive Tone Ahead of Tech Earnings
summary: This article discusses the Q1 earnings season with a focus on big tech companies. While Tesla reported a decline in earnings and revenue, Alphabet surpassed analyst estimates due to strong performance in its search and advertising units, driven by AI investments. Intel also beat estimates but provided weak forward-looking guidance and announced workforce reductions.

title: NSDC skills polytechnic, ITI students in job roles related to AI, EV, renewable energy, cybersecurity
summary: The National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC), through its National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (NAPS), is training polytechnic, ITI, and engineering students in cutting-edge fields like AI, EVs, and cybersecurity to prepare them for industry demands. Over 12,000 apprentices have already participated, with many securing jobs at major companies like Google and Toyota. While the scheme aims to enroll 46 lakh apprentices by 2026 and has seen 45% secure full-time employment, challenges remain, such as low industry registration in some states and awareness issues among students.

title: TKC Stock Price, News & Analysis
summary: Turkcell Iletisim Hizmetleri A.S. (TKC) is a technology and telecommunications company based in Türkiye, operating in Türkiye, Belarus, and Northern Cyprus, with dual listings on the NYSE and Borsa Istanbul. The company focuses on mobile and fixed connectivity, digital services, and its growing Techfin segment which includes digital payments and financing. Turkcell is also strategically investing in data centers and cloud services, notably through a partnership with Google Cloud, and has secured significant 5G spectrum for network evolution.

title: Alphabet, First Citizens Bancshares, O'Reilly Automotive
summary: This article details various analyst rating and price target changes for numerous US-listed companies, including Alphabet, First Citizens Bancshares, and O'Reilly Automotive. Analysts from firms such as Wedbush, JP Morgan, RBC, and others have updated their outlooks on these companies, with some raising and others lowering their price targets. The report highlights significant adjustments across different sectors and company sizes.

title: BRZE Stock Price, News & Analysis
summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of Braze, Inc. (Nasdaq: BRZE), a software company specializing in a customer engagement platform. It details Braze's core platform capabilities, its significant focus on AI through BrazeAI™, data personalization features, and its industry positioning as a leader in marketing technology. The article also covers Braze's business model, financial characteristics, stock information, and social impact initiatives.

title: Woogle: The fake merger that proves we need zero trust
summary: The author conducted a social experiment by posting a fake announcement on LinkedIn about a fictional "Woogle" merger between Google and Wiz. This prank, which included a harmless external link, resulted in over 2,500 clicks within four weeks, demonstrating how easily professionals can fall for deceptive links. The article uses this experiment to highlight the critical need for a Zero Trust security model, emphasizing that human curiosity and trust are not sufficient defenses against potential cybersecurity threats.

title: Google Parent Alphabet Leads Adtech Stocks Higher on Signs of AI Success
summary: Alphabet's shares rose after its quarterly earnings beat expectations, driven by the success of AI features like AI Overviews in Google Search. This positive performance also lifted other adtech and AI-related stocks, and analysts have increased price targets for Alphabet, anticipating further growth in AI-driven services. Several other tech companies with cloud and digital advertising businesses are set to report earnings soon, potentially reflecting similar trends.

title: Alphabet Earnings: Solid Results as AI Monetization Continues to Improve
summary: Alphabet reported solid first-quarter 2025 results with 12% sales growth and 230 basis points operating margin improvement year-over-year, driven significantly by Google Cloud's 28% growth. The company demonstrates clear progress in AI monetization across various platforms, leading Morningstar to maintain its $237 fair value estimate and view the stock as materially undervalued. Despite antitrust concerns and a turbulent macro environment, Alphabet's continued execution and strategic AI investments are highlighted as key strengths.

summary: Morgan Stanley analysts project that Google Cloud's revenue could expand by over 50% in 2026, driven by an updated backlog model. Analyst Brian Nowak notes that Google Cloud is seen as a key driver for GOOGL's multiple expansion and AI-driven outperformance. This growth is supported by a strong backlog and consistent growth in on-demand workloads.

title: Google's Internet Traffic Share in Korea Hits Record 31%, Fees Unpaid
summary: Google's internet traffic share in Korea has reached a record 31.17%, the highest for two consecutive years, yet the company continues to avoid paying network usage fees amounting to hundreds of billions of won. This places a significant burden on Korean telecom companies, which are struggling to meet the rising demand for network investment due to increased traffic from streaming services. Despite growing calls for government and industry intervention to address Google's "network free-riding," factors like potential trade friction, a political vacuum, and an SK Telecom hacking incident have stalled efforts.

title: Alphabet: Testimony From Apple Search Executive Rattles Investors
summary: Apple's Eddy Cue testified in the DOJ lawsuit against Alphabet, indicating Apple may end its long-term Google Search partnership for Safari. This news caused investor concern, given Google's significant revenue-sharing agreement with Apple. Despite potential changes and AI's impact on search, Morningstar maintains its fair value estimate for Alphabet, believing the market's reaction is overblown and that Google will adapt with AI-infused offerings.

title: Google's parent company Alphabet shares plunge over 8% amid AI search competition, malware threats
summary: Alphabet Inc. shares dropped over 8% following revelations from an antitrust trial, citing increased AI search competition and new malware threats. Apple executive Eddy Cue testified about declining Google search traffic due to AI alternatives and announced future AI choices in Safari. Google also disclosed a new malware strain, LOSTKEYS, linked to a Russian hacking group, and partnered with Elementl Power to build nuclear plants to power AI data centers, addressing the anticipated surge in electricity usage.

title: Prudential bets on AI, but can tech fix its performance?
summary: Prudential is leveraging AI and digital transformation to overcome past performance issues, with Group CIO Ashley Veasey leading the charge. The insurer's strategy focuses on digital health services, advanced tools for agents and customers, and an AI Lab in Singapore producing over 100 use cases. Despite challenges like legacy systems and varying market infrastructure, Prudential aims for technology to drive sustained business results, higher returns on equity, and improved customer satisfaction in Asia and Africa.

title: Nutanix reveals cloud cluster solution alongside Google Cloud
summary: Nutanix is partnering with Google Cloud to release Nutanix Cloud Clusters (NC2), a solution designed to simplify hybrid multicloud infrastructure. This offering helps companies modernize legacy systems and migrate applications across clouds, particularly addressing the growing demand for Google Cloud adoption and AI workload management. The collaboration leverages Google's bare metal service to provide customers with more choice and agility in hybrid cloud environments.

title: Dotdash Meredith’s Earnings Are Flat, Following Google Grief And Tariff Trouble
summary: Dotdash Meredith (DDM) reported flat ad revenue growth for Q1, despite its contextual targeting platform D/Cipher, as it faces challenges from Google's AI-powered search results and tariff-induced economic uncertainty. The company saw a decline in core user sessions due to Google's AI Overviews but experienced growth in performance marketing and licensing revenue through partnerships with OpenAI and syndication deals. Macroeconomic headwinds, particularly from Trump tariffs affecting programmatic ad pricing and major Chinese buyers like Temu and Shein, pose further threats to DDM's advertising business.

title: Pinterest updates visual search with more AI-powered features
summary: Pinterest is enhancing its visual search capabilities with new AI-powered features. These updates aim to improve search precision, make visual search more accessible, and help users identify aesthetic preferences by generating descriptive words. The company hopes these advancements will solidify Pinterest's role as a visual shopping tool, leveraging AI to improve user experience rather than detract from it.

title: Jim Cramer Surprised Alphabet (GOOGL) AI “Didn’t Cannibalize Google” — Still “Doesn’t Care for the Stock”
summary: Jim Cramer expressed surprise that Alphabet's (GOOGL) AI product, Gemini, did not negatively impact its core Google search revenues, despite his personal disinterest in the stock. He lauded the resilience of large technology companies, highlighting their scale, financial strength, and ability to adapt to new challenges like AI. Cramer also emphasized the importance of supporting these "super stocks" against legal challenges.

title: Strategy's Saylor Reveals Asset That Leaves Bitcoin in Dust This Year
summary: Michael Saylor of Strategy revealed that his company's stock (MSTR) has significantly outperformed Bitcoin and other major assets in 2025, growing by 249% compared to Bitcoin's 64%. Despite MSTR's superior performance, Saylor continues to advocate for accumulating Bitcoin, citing Strategy's own substantial BTC acquisitions and growth since adopting the Bitcoin standard. The company recently purchased 15,355 BTC and plans to raise $42 billion to buy even more.

title: Apple clears Spotify update under new rules allowing purchases within apps
summary: Apple has approved Spotify's U.S. app update, allowing users to access pricing information and make purchases directly within the app, following a U.S. federal judge's order against Apple's in-app commission policy. This change enables Spotify users to see pricing, purchase subscriptions, and use alternative payment methods. Apple, despite disagreeing and planning to appeal, stated it will comply with the court's decision, marking a significant development for developers seeking fairer competition.

title: Lumen promises to bypass Equinix
summary: Lumen Technologies has launched a new "fabric" port offering designed to allow enterprise customers to connect to various digital services and cloud providers through a single port, aiming to bypass data center interconnect companies like Equinix. Lumen CEO Kate Johnson stated this disruptive service would eliminate the need for costly cross-connect fees. While some analysts view this as an innovative move to simplify cloud connections and reduce costs for enterprises, others caution that breaking into Equinix's established business model will be challenging given its broad reach, security benefits, and efficient multi-cloud access.

title: O’Reilly Automotive, Inc. (ORLY): Among Charles Akre’s and John Neff’s Stock Picks With Huge Upside Potential
summary: This article highlights O’Reilly Automotive, Inc. (ORLY) as a key holding in Charles Akre’s and John Neff’s portfolios, emphasizing its significant upside potential. It discusses Akre Capital Management's investment philosophy, focusing on high-quality growth stocks, and details ORLY's strong market position, consistent sales growth, and future expansion plans, noting its 9th rank on a list of high-potential stock picks from Akre Capital.

title: Meta Platforms is nearing new highs on a wave of potential acquisition reports 📈
summary: Meta Platforms is nearing new highs as reports surface about its potential acquisition of PlayAI, an AI startup specializing in voice imitation. This move is seen as part of Meta's broader strategy to lead in artificial intelligence development, following a significant investment in Scale AI and bringing its CEO into Meta's "superintelligence" team in mid-June. The company's stock prices are consequently approaching all-time highs as it strives to outpace competitors like Alphabet, Microsoft, and Amazon in the AI race.

title: Amazon AWS exec leaves company as tech talent race heats up (AMZN:NASDAQ)
summary: Vasi Philomin, a vice president of generative AI at Amazon AWS, has departed from the company, as reported by Reuters. Amazon confirmed Philomin is no longer an employee. This departure highlights the intensifying competition for tech talent, particularly in the generative AI space.

title: Indonesia working on new e-commerce tax rule in bid to target 'shadow economy'
summary: Indonesia is implementing a new regulation requiring e-commerce platforms to collect a 0.5% levy on sellers' sales income to combat the "shadow economy." The move, which aims to improve supervision and tax compliance, has received backing from consulted stakeholders, though platforms and the e-commerce association are requesting adequate time for implementation due to the significant number of sellers affected.

title: Apple to expand its footprint in Seattle with office lease
summary: Apple is significantly expanding its presence in Seattle by signing a lease for a 660,000-square-foot office space at 333 Dexter. This move is expected to add an additional 2,000 jobs to the area over the next five years, building upon a previously announced plan to create 1,000 jobs and a new $1 billion campus in Austin, Texas. The expansion highlights Seattle's growing tech influence and talent pool, attracting major technology firms like Google and Facebook as well.

title: Coursera Named a Leader in Technology Skills Development Platforms Evaluation
summary: Coursera has been recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™: Technology Skills Development Platforms, Q2 2025, receiving the maximum possible score in eight criteria including AI Capabilities for Skills Acquisition. The evaluation highlighted Coursera's high-quality content, rapid adaptation to technology trends (like AI), strong partner ecosystem, and powerful analytics. This recognition validates Coursera's commitment to providing world-class, relevant, and scalable learning experiences to help organizations and individuals build essential technology skills.

title: Better Home Hit With Lawsuit Over Google, LinkedIn Web Trackers
summary: Better Home & Finance Holding Co. is facing a proposed class action lawsuit for allegedly using web trackers from Google and LinkedIn to collect customer data without consent. The lawsuit claims that the real estate and mortgage company shared sensitive personal and financial information, such as home addresses and credit scores, which customers expected to remain private. The complaint was filed in the US District Court for the Central District of California, on behalf of potentially millions of affected individuals.

summary: Tesla's Robotaxi service successfully launched in Austin, causing TSLA stock to rise by 3% in five days. summary: Faraday Future announced the debut of FF AI 2.0, a significant software update built with OpenAI, for its FF 91 and future FX models. This intelligent in-car OS offers enhanced natural language understanding, real-time multilingual switching in over 50 languages, and a multi-agent AI architecture. The update aims to provide a new class of intelligent driving experiences and is designed to integrate large language models across core vehicle systems.

title: WISeSat.Space and SEALSQ Launch New Satellite with SpaceX,
summary: SEALSQ's subsidiary, WISeSat.Space, has launched its WISeSat 3 satellite aboard SpaceX's Falcon 9, marking the first-ever Decentralized Physical Infrastructure Network (DePIN) from orbit and advancing quantum-safe space communications. The satellite incorporates SEALSQ's Quantum RootKey and enables SEALCOIN token exchanges, establishing a new era for cybersecurity and decentralized finance in space. This initiative strengthens Europe's position in space technology and promotes independence in the quantum computing age.

title: Tesla stock soars following Austin AI robotaxi event
summary: Tesla officially launched its AI robotaxi service in Austin, Texas, charging a $4.20 flat fee for initial rides. The debut caused Tesla's stock to jump over 10%, highlighting the long-awaited entry into the robotaxi market. The article also details the company's future plans, including the CyberCab sedan and expansion to other cities, and compares Tesla's new service with established players like Waymo and Zoox.

title: Market Update: The Section 899 “revenge tax”
summary: The article discusses Section 899, a new foreign tax provision in the proposed One Big Beautiful Bill Act, aimed at discouraging what the U.S. considers "unfair foreign taxes" on American companies. This "revenge tax" would impose a levy on entities from nations with tax policies deemed discriminatory towards U.S. businesses, potentially impacting dividends and corporate profits starting in 2027. While it could raise revenue and create a more level playing field, the tax might also deter investment in the U.S. and shift equity investors to international markets, with specific implications for high-dividend U.S. companies and those with significant U.S. operations.

title: Accenture is worst-performing stock in the S&P 500 on Friday
summary: Accenture became the worst-performing stock in the S&P 500 on Friday, despite reporting strong revenue and profits with optimistic guidance. The decline was attributed to investor concerns over a 6% decrease in third-quarter bookings, which totaled $19.7 billion. Shares of Accenture Plc Class were down 8.2% and on track for their biggest drop since March 2024, contributing to a 20% year-to-date fall.

title: NetEase Games
summary: NetEase Games has launched Custom Servers for "Once Human," allowing players to create personalized game experiences with customizable parameters and scenarios. Additionally, the company announced the "Once Human Global Annual Version 2.0 Showcase" for July 2nd, 2025, which will reveal future game plans, including new scenarios and updates for the PvP spin-off, "Once Human: Raidzone." Pre-registration for Version 2.0 is open, offering exclusive in-game rewards.

title: Pichai Family Foundation to Sell 4,000 Alphabet Shares Worth $708K
summary: The Pichai Family Foundation has filed a notice to sell 4,000 shares of Alphabet Inc.'s Class C common stock, valued at approximately $708,920. This transaction is part of a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted in December 2024, and the foundation has made similar sales totaling 4,000 shares on five previous occasions in the last three months. The shares were originally acquired through restricted stock units granted in April 2018.

title: Sundar Pichai Sales Alphabet Stock Worth $5.6 Million
summary: Sundar Pichai, CEO of Alphabet Inc., filed a notice to sell 32,500 shares of the company's Class C common stock, valued at nearly $5.76 million. This transaction, conducted under Rule 144, is part of a series of regular stock sales by Pichai and his family foundation under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. Over the past three months, Pichai has personally sold 130,000 shares totaling approximately $21.78 million, with an additional $3.24 million from the Pichai Family Foundation's sale of 20,000 shares.

title: Meta platforms CEO Zuckerberg sells $1.93 million in shares
summary: Mark Zuckerberg, CEO of Meta Platforms, sold $1.93 million in Class A Common Stock on June 18, 2025, through two transactions conducted by the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative Foundation under a prearranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. title: Tech Tactically Trimmed: Why I'm Holding MAGS But With Caution
summary: The author remains bullish on big tech long-term but advises caution due to current Magnificent 7 valuations and macro headwinds. Meintzer discusses the impact of a new CEO, the firewall refresh cycle, the shift towards platformization, and Check Point's M&A strategy, including the recent Verity acquisition and partnership with Whiz. The conversation also covers the company's long-term growth drivers, particularly its subscription services.

title: Geoffrey Hinton Says Be A 'Plumber' If You Want To Have A Safe Job, 'Godfather Of AI' Says Everyone Doing 'Mundane Intellectual Labor' Will Be Replaced
summary: Geoffrey Hinton, known as the "Godfather of AI," advises young job seekers to consider careers in trades like plumbing, as artificial intelligence is poised to replace roles involving "mundane intellectual labor." He warns that AI copilots could lead to mass layoffs, and stresses the importance of physical dexterity, which robots still lack. Hinton, who left Google to speak openly about AI risks, highlights large-scale unemployment as an immediate threat and urges policymakers to consider universal basic income and rapid reskilling.

title: US critical infrastructure could become casualty of Iran-Israel conflict
summary: Security researchers are warning that the direct conflict between Israel and Iran could lead to an escalation of cyberattacks against U.S. critical infrastructure. Iran-aligned threat groups, including state-sponsored actors, hacktivists, and cybercriminals, may target U.S. companies and individuals with cyber espionage or sabotage. Experts recommend that critical infrastructure providers harden their defenses against both direct intrusions and supply chain attacks, especially given past incidents like Iranian hacktivists disrupting the U.S. water sector.

title: Could Amazon Become the Next Great Dividend Stock?
summary: Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) is financially capable of paying dividends, as evidenced by its strong first-quarter 2025 results, but the company currently prioritizes growth initiatives over dividend payments. While an Amazon dividend announcement is unlikely in the near future, investors seeking dividend-like income from AMZN might consider the YieldMax AMZN Option Income Strategy ETF (NYSEARCA:AMZY), which offers significant cash distributions through a covered call strategy, though it carries higher risks.

title: Introducing Quartz AI Factor, a new streaming video series set at the Nasdaq MarketSite
summary: Quartz has launched a new streaming video series, "Quartz AI Factor," filmed at the Nasdaq MarketSite, to explore how AI is transforming business. The series features interviews with industry leaders, including Doug Clinton, CEO of Intelligent Alpha, an investment firm utilizing advanced generative AI models for its strategies. Clinton emphasizes the importance of continuous innovation to stay ahead in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.

title: Google's First 'Beam' Videoconferencing Device is 'HP Dimension', Coming Late 2025 at $25,000
summary: HP has unveiled 'Dimension', its first hardware offering based on Google Beam's 3D videoconferencing platform, targeting enterprise clients at a price of $25,000. The device, which uses six cameras and AI to create realistic 3D video on a 65-inch light field display, aims to enhance human connection in virtual meetings. While marketed as a light-field display, some commentators suggest it's a sophisticated auto-stereoscopic system with advanced rendering rather than a true light-field technology.

title: Expedia and Accor CEOs: AI to Reinvent the Future of Travel
summary: Expedia CEO Ariane Gorin and Accor CEO Sébastien Bazin discussed how AI is poised to fundamentally transform the hospitality and travel industries. They detailed how their companies are implementing AI to automate back-end operations, enhance customer experiences from booking to in-trip support, and improve efficiency across various functions. Both executives stressed the importance of maintaining human involvement to ensure personalized interaction and support, particularly when travel plans go awry.

title: Oracle's stock closes out best week since 2001 on cloud momentum
summary: Oracle's stock experienced its best week since 2001, surging 24% after a strong earnings report indicated significant momentum in its cloud business. The company's CEO, Safra Catz, provided revenue guidance exceeding consensus expectations, fueled by increasing demand for Oracle's cloud infrastructure, particularly from clients needing to operate AI models. Oracle is rapidly expanding its data center capacity to meet this demand, outpacing other tech megacaps in stock performance this year.

title: GGLL Bulls Watch Willow—GGLS Bears Watch DC
summary: This article discusses the current performance of Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) stock, which has seen a significant drop after reaching an all-time high in February. It explores two potential catalysts for the stock's future: Alphabet's new Willow quantum computing chip, which could drive growth and innovation, and looming antitrust risks and U.S. export controls, which could negatively impact the company. The article also highlights Direxion's leveraged ETFs, GGLL and GGLS, for traders looking to capitalize on bullish or bearish movements in GOOGL.

title: First stage of Amazon’s 4 GWh Californian solar-plus-storage site is built
summary: AES Corp has completed the first 1 GW stage of Amazon's 4 GWh Bellefield solar-plus-storage project in California, with the second stage expected to be finished next year. The full facility will be the largest of its kind in the US, powering around 467,000 homes and offsetting over one million tons of carbon emissions annually. AES is building the project under a 15-year deal with Amazon and is also a major clean energy supplier to data center hyperscalers.

title: Forget Star Trek, HP Just Made 3D Hologram Calls A Reality With Google Beam
summary: HP has partnered with Google to launch the HP Dimension with Google Beam, an AI-powered 3D video communications solution designed to make virtual meetings feel more in-person. This system, which evolved from Project Starline, uses volumetric video and specialized light field displays to create lifelike 3D representations of participants without requiring headsets. While promising enhanced engagement and focus, the technology comes with a significant cost, priced at $25,000 per unit plus separate licensing fees for Google Beam.

title: HP Dimension with Google Beam Takes Virtual Collaboration to the Next Level at InfoComm 2025
summary: HP Inc. unveiled HP Dimension with Google Beam at InfoComm 2025, an AI-powered 3D video communication solution designed to enhance virtual collaboration by replicating in-person experiences without wearables. Developed with Google, this technology aims to create more authentic connections in distributed workforces through features like 3D imaging, natural eye contact, and spatial audio. Additionally, HP introduced Poly Studio A2 Audio Solutions, providing advanced audio for various meeting room sizes, with both products becoming available to customers in late 2025.

title: Meta Losing AI Spending War To Google, Microsoft, Warns 'Big Short' Investor Steve Eisman
summary: Steve Eisman, the investor known for predicting the 2008 collapse, warns that Meta Platforms Inc. is losing the AI spending war against Alphabet Inc.'s Google and Microsoft Corp. He attributes this to Meta's lack of a cloud business, which prevents it from generating revenue from its AI investments unlike Google and Microsoft, whose cash reserves are increasing while Meta's is plummeting. Despite this, Eisman believes the massive spending from all three tech giants indicates the AI boom is far from over.

title: Cineverse Announces Commercial Availability of cineSearch for Business - a Revolutionary, AI-Powered Tool that Solves Search & Discovery for Digital Platforms and Streaming Services
summary: Cineverse has announced the commercial availability of cineSearch for Business, an AI-powered content search and discovery tool for digital platforms and streaming services. This tool, developed using Google Cloud's Vertex AI and Gemini 2.0 Pro, aims to improve viewer search experiences, user retention, and time spent on platforms by offering personalized and superior content recommendations. CineSearch for Business is now available for licensing to OEMs and streaming platforms, addressing industry challenges such as poor discovery leading to subscriber cancellations and long search times.

title: Evogene Announces Completion of First-In-Class Foundation Model for Generative Molecule Design, Developed in Collaboration with Google Cloud
summary: Evogene announced the completion of its first-in-class generative AI foundation model for small molecule design, developed in collaboration with Google Cloud. This new model, integrated with Evogene's ChemPass AI, addresses the challenge of identifying novel small molecules that meet multiple complex product criteria for pharmaceutical and agriculture applications. The company reports approximately 90% precision in novel molecule designs and plans for version 2.0 with enhanced multi-parameter optimization.

title: Apple elevates the iPhone experience with iOS 26
summary: Apple previewed iOS 26, a major update featuring a new "Liquid Glass" design and powerful Apple Intelligence capabilities. Key enhancements include Live Translation, visual intelligence for screen interactions, and new ways to express oneself with Genmoji and Image Playground. The update also brings significant improvements to Phone, Messages, CarPlay, Apple Music, Maps, Wallet, and introduces a new Apple Games app.

title: Apple Inc. (AAPL): CEO Tim Cook Is The “Best Mover,” Says Jim Cramer
summary: Jim Cramer has praised Apple Inc. CEO Tim Cook as the "best mover" rather than a first mover, highlighting his strategic ability to perfect existing ideas. Cramer acknowledges potential challenges for Apple, including concerns about its AI development relative to competitors and the implications of a legal battle with Epic Games. Despite these issues, Cramer views AAPL as a favorable investment and lists it as 3rd among stocks he discusses, though he suggests some AI stocks might offer greater short-term returns.

title: Apple, X, and Airbnb among growing number of Big Tech firms exploring crypto adoption
summary: A growing number of Big Tech firms, including Apple, X (formerly Twitter), Airbnb, and Google, are exploring the adoption of stablecoins to reduce transaction costs and optimize cross-border payments. This interest is driven by a perceived shift in the regulatory environment towards blockchain technology and early successes by companies like Google Cloud in integrating stablecoin payments. While discussions are initial for some, the industry sees stablecoins as a significant upgrade to traditional payment systems.

title: Walmart Inc. Signs 338,000-SF Lease at Jay Paul Company’s Iconic Tech Corners Campus in Sunnyvale
summary: Walmart Inc.'s eCommerce division has signed a 338,307-square-foot lease at Jay Paul Company’s Tech Corners in Sunnyvale, California, marking the largest new office lease in Silicon Valley since 2023. This expansion strengthens Walmart's presence in the region within a modernized, wellness-focused campus. The transaction highlights robust momentum in Silicon Valley's office leasing market, particularly in Sunnyvale.

title: How Nvidia's $10 Ripple Effect Could Lift Adobe, Salesforce, ServiceNow
summary: Nvidia's AI chip dominance creates a ripple effect, with Wedbush analyst Dan Ives stating that for every $1 spent on Nvidia, there's an $8-$10 multiplier across the tech ecosystem. This benefits software giants like Adobe, Salesforce, and ServiceNow as enterprises shift from AI infrastructure buildouts to deployment. Wedbush believes 2025 will be an inflection point for enterprise AI adoption, making these software companies key beneficiaries of the $2 trillion AI spending wave.

title: Plus, an AI-Based Virtual Driver Software Company Powering Factory-Built Autonomous Trucks, to Go Public via Merger with Churchill Capital Corp IX
summary: Plus, an AI-based virtual driver software company for autonomous trucks, announced its plan to go public via a merger with Churchill Capital Corp IX. The transaction is expected to provide up to $300 million in gross proceeds to fund Plus through the commercial launch of its SuperDrive-enabled, factory-built autonomous trucks in 2027, valuing Plus at $1.2 billion pre-money equity. This strategic move aims to address the $2 trillion trucking freight market by leveraging Physical AI and OEM partnerships with companies like TRATON GROUP, Hyundai, and IVECO.

title: Uber adds Palo Alto CEO Nikesh Arora to its board after executive shakeup
summary: Nikesh Arora, CEO of Palo Alto Networks, is joining Uber's board of directors following an executive shakeup at the ride-hailing company. This move comes as Uber faces increasing competition in the robotaxi and food delivery sectors and is actively partnering with companies like Waymo for autonomous mobility. Arora's appointment is expected to help Uber navigate its strategic direction, especially in autonomous technology.

title: Amazon.com (AMZN) Turns Up the Heat in CTV Ad Wars With Fresh DSP Discounts
summary: Amazon (AMZN) is intensifying competition in the Connected TV (CTV) advertising market by offering discounts on its Demand-Side Platform (DSP) to attract marketers. While Google is using ad credits for its DV360 service, Amazon's aggressive strategy aims to capitalize on the growing CTV advertising momentum. Despite strong competition from The Trade Desk, Amazon is closely watched by investors as it navigates the digital advertising landscape.

title: CXApp Inc. - SEC.gov
summary: CXApp Inc. (Nasdaq: CXAI) reported strong Q1 2025 customer success metrics, highlighting a 99% subscription revenue ratio and 88% gross margin. The company secured 130%+ expansion renewals with three large clients and is seeing increased adoption of its Agentic AI-based CXAI platform, particularly the Kiosk and VU products, for Return-to-Office initiatives. CXApp anticipates continued profitable growth driven by its subscription model and advanced AI solutions for employee workplace experiences.

title: Google cuts trust in Chunghwa Telecom's certificates: What's at stake for internet security chain?
summary: Google has decided to remove default trust for digital certificates issued by Chunghwa Telecom starting July 31, 2025, due to "longstanding patterns of compliance failures." This move, following Mozilla Firefox's earlier consideration of delisting, has significant implications for Chunghwa Telecom, which dominates Taiwan's market, and highlights broader issues in internet security and certificate authority governance. The article explores the function of digital certificates, why browsers can unilaterally delist CAs, and the potential fallout for businesses and users.

title: Top Active Managers Love These ETFs’ Holdings
summary: This article highlights that top active managers are keen on the holdings of ETFs like the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and the Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM). Despite a 2025 pullback, these managers leveraged first-quarter retrenchment in mega-cap growth stocks like Alphabet, Amazon, Microsoft, and Nvidia to increase their positions, signaling strong confidence in these companies' fundamental outlooks, especially in areas like cloud computing and advertising. The ETFs allocate about 28% of their rosters to these popular names, suggesting potential benefits for QQQ/QQQM investors.

title: Spotify Stock Smashes Records: Goldman Sees $200 Billion Music Streaming Market By 2035
summary: Spotify's stock hit a record high after a Goldman Sachs report projected the global music market will nearly double to $197 billion by 2035, driven largely by emerging markets. Goldman revised its compound annual growth rate forecast, highlighting that while growth may slow, new monetization strategies, particularly in superfan-targeted products and concert ticketing, will be key. Spotify, which holds a significant market share, is expected to benefit from these trends, along with strategic price hikes and product innovation.

title: Google Chrome to distrust newer Chunghwa Telecom digital certificates
summary: Google Chrome will stop automatically trusting new digital certificates issued by Chunghwa Telecom after July 31, 2025, due to "compliance failures." Chunghwa Telecom stated that this decision is not linked to any cybersecurity issues. The full story is available in the CNA English news archive for subscribers.

title: News Corp influenced the Election, just not the way it wanted
summary: Dr. Victoria Fielding argues that News Corp significantly influenced the recent Australian election, but not in its intended way. By promoting a hard-right, "Trumpist" political agenda within the Liberal and National parties, News Corp inadvertently contributed to a public backlash that led to Labor's victory. The article highlights News Corp's pervasive media influence and its deep entanglement in right-wing Australian politics, suggesting that its efforts to push a specific political ideology ultimately backfired with the electorate.

title: Google Chrome to distrust Chunghwa Telecom, Netlock certificates in August
summary: Google Chrome will distrust root CA certificates from Chunghwa Telecom and Netlock starting August 1, 2025, due to persistent compliance failures. This change will cause "Your connection is not private" warnings for websites using their certificates, prompting web administrators to switch to trusted Certificate Authorities. This action follows Google's increased scrutiny of CAs and similar decisions against other providers.

title: Chrome to Distrust Chunghwa Telecom and Netlock Certificates
summary: Google announced that its Chrome Root Store will no longer trust digital certificates issued by Chunghwa Telecom and Netlock due to "patterns of concerning behavior observed over the past year." This change will take effect in Chrome 139 for certificates issued after July 31, 2025, and website operators are advised to replace affected certificates to avoid security warnings for users. Google emphasizes that this decision is to preserve the integrity of the Chrome Root Store and ensure user safety.

title: Procter & Gamble Earnings: What To Look For From PG
summary: Procter & Gamble (PG) is set to report earnings, with analysts expecting revenue to grow by 1.6% to $20.85 billion and adjusted earnings of $1.42 per share. Despite some positive sentiment in the consumer staples sector, analysts have grown increasingly bearish on PG's revenue estimates. The company has a history of missing Wall Street revenue projections, and its stock price has remained flat over the last month, heading into earnings below the average analyst price target.

title: Duke Energy’s AI Strategy: Analysis of Dominance in Utilities AI
summary: This report analyzes how Duke Energy's AI strategy positions it for dominance in the utilities sector, especially with the surge in electricity demand from generative AI. Duke Energy's massive scale, regulated business model, and aggressive $83 billion capital plan, coupled with strategic partnerships and innovative regulatory frameworks, create a unique competitive advantage. The company is actively implementing AI across its operations for grid modernization, predictive maintenance, and efficient management of renewable energy, distinguishing its approach from competitors focused on more siloed AI applications.

title: T-Mobile Launches Its Cellular Starlink Service: What You Need to Know
summary: T-Mobile is officially launching its cellular Starlink service, "T-Satellite," making satellite connectivity available to smartphone users in dead zones for a monthly fee after a free beta period. While T-Mobile subscribers on premium plans get it free, others, including users on rival carriers, can subscribe for $10 per month, primarily for SMS and location sharing, with MMS and select app data support rolling out soon. This integration allows Google search advertisers to place sponsored product listings across Criteo’s network of over 200 retailers and e-commerce merchants, tapping into search budgets that traditionally stayed outside retail media. Criteo sees this as a crucial first step in a broader enterprise arrangement with Google, with potential future integrations with other Google marketing platform tools like DV360.

This partnership will enable Criteo's network of over 200 retailers to receive demand from Google Search Ads 360, allowing advertisers to create and optimize campaigns while benefiting from unified measurement capabilities. The collaboration aims to democratize the retail media landscape, projected to reach $204 billion by 2027, by empowering more diverse retailers to attract advertising budgets.

title: Tesco to Victoria's Secret: India's booming back-offices worry about rising trade barriers

The suite includes an AI team lead named Vera, which coordinates specialized agents to automate complex tasks, generate reports, and summarize financial data, thereby freeing professionals from repetitive work.

title: Qualys, Tenable Latest Victims of Salesloft Drift Hack

This attack involves the theft of OAuth authentication tokens from Salesloft Drift, a third-party application integrated with Salesforce, leading to unauthorized access to customer information stored in Salesforce instances. The Google Threat Intelligence Group first identified this ongoing campaign, which has affected a growing list of companies including Google, BeyondTrust, Cloudflare, and Palo Alto Networks.

He alleges these companies are understating depreciation by artificially extending the "useful life" of their compute assets despite rapidly evolving hardware. title: Tune In: Understanding Google v. Sonos and its Patent Prosecution Implications
summary: The U.S. Federal Circuit's decision in Google LLC v. Sonos, Inc. on prosecution laches provided crucial guidance, limiting the doctrine's applicability for post-1995 patents. This ruling prompts patent practitioners to strategically signal future claim intentions in initial filings to mitigate laches risks.

This strategic hire underscores the bank's commitment to advancing its artificial intelligence capabilities to innovate financial services and improve operations for employees, customers, and communities. Shafiq brings over 15 years of technology leadership experience from companies like Amazon Web Services and Google, and will oversee the vision, roadmap, and development of enterprise-wide AI-powered products.

title: Sabre’s Agentic AI: Augmenting Travel Agents in a Chat-Driven Era
summary: Sabre is spearheading the integration of agentic AI into the travel industry, aiming to augment rather than displace human travel agents. Through innovative tools like its Model Context Protocol (MCP) server and agentic-ready APIs, Sabre is enabling AI to automate complex tasks such as booking and itinerary management, fostering a collaborative ecosystem between humans and AI. Partnerships and pilot programs, including a significant alliance with BizTrip AI, are paving the way for broader adoption and a redefined role for travel agencies focused on high-touch service and complex problem-solving.

title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang Calls Anthropic's Claude 'Incredible,' Says Every Software Company Needs To Use It

Huang detailed AI as a five-layer infrastructure stack and revealed that Nvidia utilizes Claude extensively within its own operations. This endorsement comes amidst reports of significant investments in Anthropic from Microsoft and Nvidia.

title: Elon Musk kills Tesla Autopilot to push Full Self-Driving subscriptions
summary: Tesla has removed its basic Autopilot software as a standard feature on new Model Y and Model 3 purchases in the US, now only offering Traffic-Aware Cruise Control. This move is intended to push customers towards the more advanced Full Self-Driving (FSD) subscriptions, which Elon Musk recently announced would shift from an $8,000 one-time fee to a monthly subscription, with prices potentially rising as FSD capabilities improve. The change comes amidst regulatory scrutiny over Tesla's marketing of Autopilot and FSD, and coincides with the company rolling out "unsupervised" robotaxi rides in Austin as part of a push to meet compensation plan milestones tied to FSD subscribers.

While this offers a significant pipeline and relationship boost, the company still faces challenges such as modest revenue growth forecasts, a high earnings multiple, recent leadership changes, and insider selling.

title: Remote Code Execution With Modern AI/ML Formats and Libraries
summary: Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 identified remote code execution (RCE) vulnerabilities in three open-source AI/ML Python libraries: NVIDIA's NeMo, Salesforce's Uni2TS, and Apple/EPFL's FlexTok. These vulnerabilities arise from the libraries' use of metadata to configure complex models, which, when loaded, execute embedded malicious code. Although no in-the-wild exploits have been found, the affected vendors have released fixes, and Palo Alto Networks products offer protection.

title: Radware Uncovers ZombieAgent, a Zero-Click AI Vulnerability in OpenAI Agents
summary: Radware has discovered "ZombieAgent," a zero-click indirect prompt injection vulnerability affecting OpenAI's Deep Research agent. ZombieAgent can remain persistent by implanting malicious rules in an agent's long-term memory and can autonomously propagate across an organization.
